description: Real, verifiable stories of resilience and comeback from public figures.

topics.title: J.K. Rowling — 12 rejections before Harry Potter
topics.context: Single mother on welfare in Edinburgh, 1990-1997. Rejected by Bloomsbury 12 times before an 8-year-old daughter of the CEO championed the book. Focus on the specific mechanics of rejection and word-of-mouth breakthrough. Avoid unverified quotes.

topics.title: Colonel Sanders — 1,009 restaurant rejections before KFC
topics.context: Harland Sanders was 62, on a $105 monthly Social Security check, and driving state to state pitching his fried chicken recipe. Well-documented in his 1974 autobiography. Focus on the age factor and persistence framing.

topics.title: Vera Wang — Started fashion at 40
topics.context: Failed to make the 1968 US Olympic figure skating team, worked at Vogue for 17 years, was passed over for Editor-in-Chief. Launched her bridal empire at 40. Verifiable timeline; focus on late-start narrative.

topics.title: Steven Spielberg — Rejected from USC film school twice
topics.context: Rejected by USC's School of Cinematic Arts twice before landing at Cal State Long Beach. Won 3 Academy Awards. In 1994 USC gave him an honorary degree; in 1996 he joined the board of trustees. Well-documented.

topics.title: Oprah Winfrey — Fired from her first TV job
topics.context: Fired from Baltimore's WJZ-TV in 1977 as an evening news anchor, told she was 'unfit for television.' Moved to a morning talk show format, which became her breakthrough. Focus on the pivot, not the setback.
